deep_dive.title: Phase model analysis of the effect of M-current on neural synchrony in hippocampal networks
deep_dive.abstract: Neural assemblies, transiently coordinated groups of neurons, observed in the hippocampus are thought to underlie the formation of episodic memories. Acetylcholine (ACh), a neuromodulator, that is received by the hippocampus, plays a critical role in memory and learning. A well supported hypothesis suggests that high levels of ACh during active exploration and rapid eye movement (REM) sleep promote memory encoding, while low levels during quiet waking and slow-wave sleep (SWS) support memory consolidation. We study this bidirectional role of ACh in neural assembly formation through its effect on the synchrony among neurons. We consider a network model of pyramidal neurons, each equipped with a slow, voltage-dependent, non-inactivating potassium current (M-current), which is downregulated in the presence of ACh. Neural assemblies are represented as cluster solutions to this system. Using a one-dimensional phase model reduction of a pair of weakly coupled pyramidal neurons under different levels of the M-current, we predict the symmetric cluster solutions that may emerge in larger networks equipped with all-to-all globally homogeneous, symmetric distance-dependent and nearest-neighbours coupling architectures. We find that under low ACh conditions, the network can fully synchronize, whereas high levels can desynchronize the network into multiple stable symmetric cluster solutions representing distinct neural assemblies.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: phase model of M-current effect on hippocampal synchrony during REM and SWS
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: M电流对海马同步性的相模型分析，关联REM和慢波睡眠
deep_dive.llm_evidence: M电流对海马同步性的相模型分析，关联REM和慢波睡眠
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: M电流对海马同步性的相模型分析，关联REM和慢波睡眠
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses a phase model to study how the M-current influences neural synchrony in hippocampal networks, linking acetylcholine levels to memory encoding in REM and consolidation in slow-wave sleep.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 海马神经群暂时性协调形成记忆痕迹，乙酰胆碱通过调节M电流影响网络同步性。本研究建立包含M电流的锥体神经元网络相模型，分析乙酰胆碱水平变化如何调控神经同步。结果显示高ACh促进REM相关同步支持编码，低ACh促进慢波睡眠同步支持巩固，揭示了M电流在睡眠阶段依赖性记忆处理中的关键作用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 海马神经群暂时性协调形成记忆痕迹，乙酰胆碱通过调节M电流影响网络同步性。本研究建立包含M电流的锥体神经元网络相模型，分析乙酰胆碱水平变化如何调控神经同步。结果显示高ACh促进REM相关同步支持编码，低ACh促进慢波睡眠同步支持巩固，揭示了M电流在睡眠阶段依赖性记忆处理中的关键作用。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: neural mechanisms of rapid eye movement sleep

deep_dive.title: Deep Sleep Classification via EEG Signal Criticality: A Passive BCI Approach for Sleep-Improvement Neurofeedback
deep_dive.abstract: Automated sleep staging is a fundamental application of passive Brain-Computer Interfaces (pBCI), decoding spontaneous neural states to enable closed-loop interventions independent of user intent. This study evaluates criticality features derived from Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) for the specific identification of deep sleep (N3).   We analyzed $347,232$ EEG epochs from $290$ older women using UMAP manifold learning to visualize state transitions. Subsequently, six classifiers were benchmarked via 10-fold cross-validation, using balanced accuracy to determine the optimal "state-sensing" engine for neurofeedback.Naive Bayes achieved the highest mean balanced accuracy ($87.17\% \pm 0.24\%$), significantly outperforming a fully connected deep neural network (FNN: $81.58\%$) and Random Forest ($80.97\%$). Linear models (LDA: $57.21\%$; SVM: $51.01\%$) performed poorly, indicating that DFA-derived criticality features reside on a distinct, non-linear manifold.   Probabilistic decoding of EEG criticality provides a high-accuracy sensing mechanism for pBCIs. This robust classification pipeline supports the development of state-dependent neurofeedback, such as targeted auditory stimulation, to enhance cognitive recovery.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Deep sleep classification using EEG criticality for sleep improvement neurofeedback
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用EEG临界性进行深度睡眠分类以实现睡眠改善神经反馈
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用EEG临界性进行深度睡眠分类以实现睡眠改善神经反馈
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用EEG临界性进行深度睡眠分类以实现睡眠改善神经反馈
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study uses criticality features from EEG to classify deep sleep (N3) with high balanced accuracy, enabling neurofeedback for sleep improvement.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究基于EEG信号的临界性特征（如去趋势波动分析）进行深度睡眠（N3）分类，在347232个EEG时段和290名老年女性数据上，朴素贝叶斯分类器达到87.17%的平均平衡准确率，为睡眠改善神经反馈提供了一种有效的被动脑机接口方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究基于EEG信号的临界性特征（如去趋势波动分析）进行深度睡眠（N3）分类，在347232个EEG时段和290名老年女性数据上，朴素贝叶斯分类器达到87.17%的平均平衡准确率，为睡眠改善神经反馈提供了一种有效的被动脑机接口方法。

deep_dive.title: Interplay of sleep neural oscillations enhances coordinated memory reactivation between cortex and hippocampus
deep_dive.abstract: Memory consolidation during sleep requires coordinated reactivation of specific experiences across hippocampus and cortex. This process occurs with synchronized neural oscillations including cortical slow waves, thalamocortical spindles, and hippocampal sharp-wave ripples. While temporal coupling of these rhythms is implicated in consolidation, a fundamental question remains: do oscillations reflect a general increase in communication, or do they selectively coordinate which memories are reactivated together across regions? Here we segregated competing memory representations in primary visual cortex by training mice on two visually distinct virtual reality tracks, each restricted to one visual hemifield, rendering their representations lateralized in cortex. Using large-scale electrophysiology, we demonstrate that hippocampus and cortex coherently reactivate the same memories. Temporally, the cortical memory trace active before and after a ripple is coherent with the memory hippocampus reactivates. Crucially, this reactivation coherence is maximally enhanced by high hippocampal ripple power in concert with high cortical spindle-band power and slow oscillation trough phase localized to the dominant memory trace reactivating. Our findings establish that sleep oscillations coordinate content-specific cortico-hippocampal reactivation necessary for consolidation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: sleep neural oscillations and memory reactivation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 睡眠神经振荡与记忆再激活
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 睡眠神经振荡与记忆再激活
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 睡眠神经振荡与记忆再激活
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study shows that specific neural oscillations (slow waves, spindles, ripples) selectively coordinate which memories are reactivated during sleep, using competing memory tasks in mice.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 睡眠中记忆巩固需要海马-皮层间的协调再激活，但振荡是否选择性指导哪些记忆被共同再激活尚不明确。本研究训练小鼠在视觉皮层中竞争性记忆表征，结合虚拟现实任务，发现慢波、纺锤波和尖波涟漪的时序耦合能够选择性地协调特定记忆的再激活，揭示了睡眠振荡在记忆巩固中的关键作用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 睡眠中记忆巩固需要海马-皮层间的协调再激活，但振荡是否选择性指导哪些记忆被共同再激活尚不明确。本研究训练小鼠在视觉皮层中竞争性记忆表征，结合虚拟现实任务，发现慢波、纺锤波和尖波涟漪的时序耦合能够选择性地协调特定记忆的再激活，揭示了睡眠振荡在记忆巩固中的关键作用。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 研究睡眠神经科学领域的学术文章, especially work that connects or combines: advancements in sleep neuroscience research; neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness; neural regulation of the sleep-wake cycle; brain circuits involved in sleep control; neural mechanisms of rapid eye movement sleep; neurobiology of non-rapid eye movement sleep; recent advances in sleep neuroscience research; neural mechanisms underlying sleep wake regulation; neurobiological basis of clinical sleep disorders.

quick_skim.title: How Much Capacity Does EEG Denoising Need? Ultra-Compact Networks reveal Benchmark Saturation and Metric-Utility Gap
quick_skim.abstract: Deep learning EEG denoising architectures have scaled from tens of thousands to tens of millions of parameters, yet no prior study has isolated model capacity as the experimental variable or tested whether reconstruction metrics predict downstream neural-signal utility. We address both gaps by fixing architecture, loss, data split, and training recipe while sweeping only channel width from 1.05K to 40.26K parameters in a minimal depthwise-separable convolutional U-Net. Models were evaluated on the EEGDenoiseNet benchmark, cross-dataset BCI transfer tests, controlled baseline retraining, and downstream motor-imagery classification with five decoder families across all nine BCI Competition IV-2a subjects. Reconstruction performance saturated by 3-6.5K parameters, with post-elbow gains of at most 0.015 correlation coefficient per log10-parameter unit. An 8.46M-parameter baseline retrained under the same pipeline matched the 40.26K compact variant on EOG--a 200x parameter gap yielding no advantage--while a Patch-Transformer control reproduced the same diminishing-return shape. Downstream evaluation exposed a classifier-dependent metric-utility gap: reconstruction-optimized denoising significantly degraded CSP+LDA classification across all nine subjects and three artifact types (best denoised accuracy 0.547 vs. 0.612 noisy baseline; Bonferroni p=0.0488), persisting on naturally recorded trials (Delta=-0.047; BH-FDR q=0.0049). End-to-end neural decoders showed variable or neutral effects. Standard EEG denoising benchmarks are saturated far below current model capacity, and reconstruction metrics do not predict BCI utility. Ultra-compact models at 33-46 KB and 1.27-2.61M FLOPs/segment are practical for edge deployment. These findings argue for capacity-controlled evaluation, harder task-aware benchmarks, and mandatory downstream validation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: EEG denoising capacity analysis for neural signals
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Ultra-compact EEG denoising networks reveal benchmark saturation and a metric-utility gap, questioning large parameter counts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有脑电去噪深度学习模型参数不断增大，但未系统研究模型容量影响。本文采用最小可分离卷积U-Net，通过仅变化通道宽度（1.05K-40.26K参数）进行实验。结果表明，在多个数据集上超紧凑网络已接近饱和，且重构指标不能预测下游神经信号效用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有脑电去噪深度学习模型参数不断增大，但未系统研究模型容量影响。本文采用最小可分离卷积U-Net，通过仅变化通道宽度（1.05K-40.26K参数）进行实验。结果表明，在多个数据集上超紧凑网络已接近饱和，且重构指标不能预测下游神经信号效用。
